methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DZYNE Technologies, a growth-stage defense and security company founded by Matt McCue, has raised $80M+ to date. The broader Defense & Security sector comprises 21 peers across public and private markets, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Shield AI.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using the $80M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $96M. Peer-median analysis was not feasible; while 21 same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints within tight filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier). A broad sector median would introduce material noise given DZYNE's specific positioning, so this method was excluded.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $82M (low) to $110M (high), with a median of $96M. This range assumes continued execution at Growth-stage benchmarks and reflects typical step-up economics from the $80M capital base. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, near-term revenue growth rates, and margin trajectory—standard opacity in private defense contractors. Additional comp narrowing via operational metrics (contract value, customer count) would strengthen confidence in this midpoint estimate.
